## 4. Assets

Information assets and the classification level that drives the Confidentiality / Integrity / Availability targets used in [§8 Findings Register](#8-findings-register) risk scoring.

| Asset | ID | Classification | Description | Linked Threats |
|--------------------|------|------------|----------------------------------------|----------------------|
| RSA Private Key (JWT signing key) | A-002 | Restricted | 1024-bit RSA private key hardcoded at lib/insecurity.ts:23. Used to sign all JWTs. Committed to public GitHub repository — permanently compromised. Any party who has read the source code can forge valid admin tokens. | [F-003](#f-003) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-006](#f-006) · [F-014](#f-014) · [F-022](#f-022) · [F-028](#f-028) · [F-030](#f-030) · [F-032](#f-032) · [F-035](#f-035) · [F-036](#f-036) · [F-040](#f-040) · [F-041](#f-041) · [F-049](#f-049) |
| Encryption Keys Directory (jwt.pub, premium.key) | A-008 | Restricted | The encryptionkeys/ directory served publicly via keyServer.ts. Contains jwt.pub (RSA public key) and premium.key. Serving these files publicly exposes the public key for offline JWT analysis. | [F-003](#f-003) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-014](#f-014) · [F-028](#f-028) · [F-030](#f-030) · [F-035](#f-035) · [F-038](#f-038) · [F-040](#f-040) · [F-041](#f-041) |
| User Credentials (email + MD5 password hash) | A-001 | Confidential | All registered user email addresses and MD5-hashed passwords stored in SQLite Users table. MD5 provides no practical protection — precomputed rainbow tables can reverse most hashes in seconds. Also includes TOTP secrets for 2FA-enrolled users. | [F-003](#f-003) · [F-004](#f-004) · [F-008](#f-008) · [F-015](#f-015) · [F-020](#f-020) · [F-021](#f-021) · [F-022](#f-022) · [F-036](#f-036) · [F-043](#f-043) · [F-050](#f-050) · [F-053](#f-053) |
| Active JWT Session Tokens | A-003 | Confidential | 6-hour RS256-signed JWTs stored in the client-side localStorage and tracked server-side in the authenticatedUsers in-memory map. Tokens encode user ID, role, and basket ID. Forgeable due to committed private key. | [F-001](#f-001) · [F-002](#f-002) · [F-003](#f-003) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-014](#f-014) · [F-028](#f-028) · [F-030](#f-030) · [F-035](#f-035) · [F-040](#f-040) · [F-041](#f-041) · [F-049](#f-049) |
| Customer Order History and Basket Contents | A-004 | Confidential | SQLite tables for Orders, Baskets, BasketItems including order line items, prices, addresses, and payment card last-four digits. Subject to IDOR attacks via missing authorization on order-history and basket endpoints. | [F-004](#f-004) · [F-007](#f-007) · [F-008](#f-008) · [F-020](#f-020) · [F-021](#f-021) · [F-030](#f-030) · [F-051](#f-051) · [F-052](#f-052) · [F-053](#f-053) · [F-055](#f-055) · [F-065](#f-065) |
| User Profile Data (addresses, payment cards, memories) | A-005 | Confidential | Personal data including delivery addresses, stored payment card references, profile images, and user-created memories. Accessible via IDOR-vulnerable address/payment APIs and served without proper authorization checks. | [F-004](#f-004) · [F-007](#f-007) · [F-008](#f-008) · [F-020](#f-020) · [F-021](#f-021) · [F-030](#f-030) · [F-051](#f-051) · [F-052](#f-052) · [F-053](#f-053) · [F-059](#f-059) · [F-065](#f-065) |
| Uploaded Files (profile images, XML, YAML submissions) | A-006 | Internal | User-uploaded files stored in uploads/. Includes profile images (SSRF via URL upload), and XML/YAML files processed by the fileUpload handler with XXE enabled. Files served back without strict content-type enforcement. | [F-010](#f-010) · [F-011](#f-011) · [F-012](#f-012) · [F-014](#f-014) · [F-039](#f-039) · [F-046](#f-046) · [F-057](#f-057) · [F-059](#f-059) |
| FTP Directory (public download area) | A-007 | Internal | The ftp/ directory served with serve-index directory listing at /ftp. Contains legal.md (removed), acquisition files, and a quarantine subdirectory. Path traversal in quarantine server may expose non-public files. | [F-014](#f-014) · [F-035](#f-035) · [F-038](#f-038) · [F-040](#f-040) · [F-041](#f-041) · [F-051](#f-051) · [F-052](#f-052) |
| Application Configuration and Version Information | A-009 | Internal | Unauthenticated endpoints /rest/admin/application-configuration and /rest/admin/application-version expose detailed runtime configuration including feature flags, domain name, challenge status, and deployed version. Useful for targeted exploitation. | — |
| Access Logs | A-010 | Internal | Morgan combined-format access logs written to logs/. Served via logfileServer.ts with user-supplied filename parameter. Path traversal in the log server could expose other filesystem contents. | [F-035](#f-035) · [F-038](#f-038) · [F-040](#f-040) · [F-041](#f-041) · [F-051](#f-051) · [F-052](#f-052) |
| Challenge Solution State | A-011 | Internal | In-memory and database-backed challenge completion flags tracking which OWASP vulnerabilities have been solved. Can be manipulated via continue-code endpoints that lack authentication. | — |
| Prometheus Metrics Endpoint | A-012 | Internal | Unauthenticated /metrics endpoint exposing Express application internals including request counts, response times, endpoint mapping, and runtime behavior — useful for reconnaissance. | — |
